ogTitle: Jamboree Heights vs Jandowae: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jamboree Heights and Jandowae.
heading: Jamboree Heights vs Jandowae — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jamboree Heights, Queensland and Jandowae, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jandowae has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Jamboree Heights (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jamboree Heights or Jandowae safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jamboree Heights has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Jandowae scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jamboree Heights scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Jandowae scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jamboree Heights or Jandowae?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jandowae has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
